name: Alphalens-Reloaded (Factor Analysis Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); alphalens-reloaded fork by Stefan Jansen

performance.notes: Factor analysis over large universes can be slow; Matplotlib tearsheets

agent_readiness.notes: Quantopian alpha factor analysis. from alphalens.utils import get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns. factor_data = get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns(factor, prices). alphalens.tears.create_full_tear_sheet(factor_data). Analyzes predictive power of alpha factors: IC (information coefficient), returns by quantile, turnover, sector analysis. MultiIndex factor input (date, asset). Requires forward return periods. Pairs with Zipline for end-to-end quant research.

gotchas: MultiIndex (date, asset) factor format required — non-trivial to construct
Factor analysis tearsheets are Matplotlib-heavy, Jupyter-native
Forward return computation can be slow for large universes
best_when: Quantitative alpha factor research (IC analysis, return by quantile)
Evaluating predictive power of signals before backtesting
avoid_when: Simple return attribution (pyfolio or quantstats simpler)
summary: Alphalens-Reloaded analyzes alpha factors' predictive power: IC (information coefficient), return by quantile, turnover, sector analysis. get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns(factor, prices) then create_full_tear_sheet(factor_data). Quantopian-origin factor research toolkit. Jupyter-native tearsheets. Essential for rigorous signal validation before backtesting. Use alphalens-reloaded (maintained fork).
